profile.name: krit garg
profile.bio: Full-Stack Developer building scalable web apps with React, Next.js, Node.js, and modern databases

prs.title: Add json-filter-transform Harbor task (Easy | Data Processing | Oracl…
prs.body: This PR introduces a new Harbor task under harbor_tasks/json-filter-transform. The task challenges the agent to process a JSON file containing user data. Specifically, it must: Read /app/input.json Filter out users where age < 18 (keep users where age >= 18) Convert the name field to uppercase for the remaining users Write the final output to /app/output.json Validation Results Oracle Score: 1.0 (The reference solution successfully executes the logic dynamically and correctly generates the expected output). NOP Score: 0.0 (The environment strictly ensures that the output file does not pre-exist, meaning an inactive agent fails instantly). Ruff Lint: Passed ✅ (All Python tests are fully compliant with formatting rules) ---

prs.title: feat: add Hospital / Healthcare Management database template
prs.body:
This PR adds a new database template called **Hospital / Healthcare Management**.

The template includes 9 tables:
patients, doctors, departments, appointments, medical_records, rooms, admissions, billing, and users.

The schema is designed to support common hospital operations such as patient registration, doctor specialization, scheduling appointments, maintaining medical records, room assignments, hospital admissions, and billing.

The structure follows the same style and relationship patterns as the existing templates:
- consistent column naming
- correct primary and foreign keys
- realistic ENUM values
- singular table names
- no breaking changes to existing templates

I have tested the template to ensure there are no missing relationships or errors.  
Please let me know if any improvements or changes are needed — I would be happy to update the PR.

prs.title: Implement Shop by Category feature

labels.name: hacktoberfest-accepted
labels.description: Marked when a pull request is reviewed and accepted under Hacktoberfest.

prs.body:
This PR implements the Shop by Category feature in the header.
* Category tiles + nav links now filter products dynamically.
* Deep-linkable URLs (e.g., ?category=fruits) are supported.
* Smooth scroll added when clicking categories.
* Each category has at least one product in both Popular and Today’s Best Deals.
This makes the section fully functional, improves navigation, and enhances the shopping experience.
✅ Fixes #38

issues.title: Add new "Hospital / Healthcare Management" template
issues.body:
Hi Team 

I noticed that DrawDB includes blog, e-commerce, HR, banking, library, and university schemas, but there is no _**Hospital / Healthcare Management template**_ yet. This is one of the most commonly used schemas in DBMS courses and real-world projects.

I would love to contribute this template. It would include tables like **patients, doctors, appointments, medical_records, departments, rooms, admissions, and billing.**

I believe this would be a valuable addition because:

Healthcare systems are a very popular use case

Many students and developers search for hospital schemas

It fits well with the template collection style and complexity

Please let me know if it's okay to proceed — I would be happy to open a PR

issues.title: Add prefers-reduced-motion Support for Accessibility
issues.body:
> **Description**

The library currently enables parallax motion for all users by default, but some users have system-level motion sensitivity preferences. According to modern accessibility standards, animation-heavy components should respect the **prefers-reduced-motion** media query.

> Problem:

Users who have enabled Reduce Motion (due to vestibular or motion-related sensitivity) continue to receive full parallax animations. This may cause discomfort and does not meet recommended accessibility guidelines (WCAG).


> Expected Behavior:

If a user has prefers-reduced-motion: reduce enabled, the parallax animation should either:
be disabled, or
run with minimal/no movement.


> Actual Behavior:

The parallax effect always runs, regardless of user preference.

 


> Why This Matters

- Improves accessibility for motion-sensitive users
- Aligns the library with modern UX best practices
- Matches behavior of animation libraries such as GSAP, Framer Motion, Lottie
- No breaking changes required


**

> Proposed Solution

**

Add this check during initialization (**constructor or enable())**:

```
// Accessibility: Respect system-level reduced motion preference
if (window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches) {
    this.disabled = true;
    return;
}
```



This ensures the effect is automatically skipped for users who prefer reduced motion.
If maintainers prefer, I can also add this as an opt-in option (e.g., respectReducedMotion: true).


### I'm happy to submit a PR with the fix once approved

issues.title: Fix navbar layout and add hamburger menu for category navigation

labels.name: hacktoberfest
labels.description: General participation tag for Hacktoberfest contributions.

labels.name: hacktoberfest-2025
labels.description: Identifies issues and PRs that are part of Hacktoberfest 2025.

issues.body:
Description:
The current main navbar needs improvements to enhance navigation, especially for smaller screens. This issue focuses on fixing the navbar layout and adding a hamburger menu to allow users to easily access category links (e.g., Food, Vegetables, Dairy, Bakery, etc.) on mobile and tablet devices.

Requirements:

Fix alignment, spacing, and responsiveness of the main navbar.

Implement a hamburger menu for mobile/tablet views.

Ensure all category links are accessible through the hamburger menu.

Maintain consistent styling with the rest of the site (header, footer, dark mode if applicable).

Smooth transitions/animations for opening and closing the menu.

Benefits:

Improves navigation for mobile and smaller screens.

Makes category links easily accessible to all users.

Enhances overall user experience and mobile usability.

issues.title: Implement “Shop by Category” Feature in Header

labels.name: hacktoberfest
labels.description: General participation tag for Hacktoberfest contributions.

issues.body:
Description:
The site header includes a Shop by Category section with options like Food, Vegetables, etc., but these links are currently not functional. We should enable this feature so users can shop or browse by category.

Requirements:

Create category-specific pages/sections (e.g., food.html, vegetables.html, etc.).

Update header navigation so clicking a category redirects/filters correctly.

Maintain consistent styling with the rest of the site (header, footer, responsiveness, dark mode if applicable).

Use placeholder/dummy product listings if real product data is not available.

Benefits:

Makes the Shop by Category section in the header functional.

Allows users to quickly find products by type.

Improves navigation and enhances overall shopping experience

issues.title: Create Refunds & Returns Policy Page
issues.body:
Description:
The footer includes a link to Refunds & Returns Policy, but the page does not exist yet. We should create this page so the link is functional and provides clear information to users.

Requirements:

Create a new refunds.html (or returns.html) page.

Add structured content explaining the refund and return policy.

Match the site’s existing styling (header, footer, dark mode, responsiveness).

Ensure the footer link points correctly to this page.

Benefits:

Makes the footer link functional.

Provides users with important information about returns and refunds.

Improves overall user experience and trust.
